visual_checks: Read through the main content and assess whether the language complexity exceeds lower secondary education level
Look for long, complex sentences with multiple subordinate clauses
Check if supplemental content is provided such as summaries, illustrations, or simplified versions
Look for text alternatives like diagrams, infographics, or videos that explain complex content
Check if the page offers a simplified or easy-read version of the content
Identify whether proper names, titles, or unavoidable technical terms inflate the perceived reading level
pass_conditions: The main text content is written at or below a lower secondary education reading level
Supplemental content (summaries, illustrations, simplified versions) is provided for complex text
An easy-read or plain language version is available and linked from the complex content
Visual aids such as diagrams or infographics supplement difficult textual content
Content that must be complex (e.g., legal, scientific) provides a plain-language summary
fail_conditions: Text content exceeds lower secondary education reading level with no supplemental aids
Complex or dense language is used throughout with no simplified alternative or summary
No visual aids, summaries, or plain-language versions are provided for advanced content
The page contains only the complex version of content with no mechanism to aid comprehension
Reading level is unnecessarily high due to overly complex sentence structures that could be simplified
na_conditions: All text content is already at or below lower secondary education reading level without supplemental aids
Content consists only of proper names, titles, or technical terms that cannot be simplified
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Reading level of the content could not be reliably assessed
common_mistakes: Assuming that educated audiences do not need supplemental content for complex material
Providing a 'simple' version that is still above lower secondary reading level
Using passive voice and long sentences throughout when active voice and shorter sentences would suffice
Not providing summaries for legal terms and conditions or privacy policies
Supplemental content that is hidden or difficult to find from the main content page

examples.pass: A government policy page includes the full legal text followed by a clearly linked 'Plain Language Summary' section that explains the key points using short sentences and common vocabulary at a lower secondary reading level
examples.fail: A public health information page uses dense medical and statistical language throughout with no summary, glossary, illustrations, or simplified version — readers without advanced education cannot easily comprehend the content
examples.partial: A university research page provides a simplified abstract at the top but the main body uses highly complex academic language with no additional supplemental aids such as diagrams or a plain-language version

plain_meaning: If the content of a page requires reading ability beyond roughly a lower-secondary (about 9 years of schooling) reading level after removing proper names and titles, then either supplemental easier content or a simplified alternate version must be available. The goal is that readers with lower literacy or cognitive disabilities can access the information one way or another.
scope_applies_to: Running prose text that conveys important information
Narrative or expository passages whose comprehension is required to use the site
Whichever sections of the content carry the substantive information
scope_does_not_apply_to: Proper names and titles (excluded from the readability measurement)
Poetry, literary art, and content whose form is inherently complex
Content that is inherently technical and has no simpler equivalent
Pages whose measured reading level is already at or below lower secondary
borderline_calibration: This SC is Level AAA and is often Not Applicable when the content is already at or below the threshold.
A supplemental summary, illustrations, audio version, or simplified alternate all count as satisfying.
Do not flag technical content that is inherently specialized unless no supplemental support is offered.
If reading level is borderline, prefer Not Enough Evidence over a hard FAIL.
